oracle.established_by: Hakim's own security review flagged both as non-blocking recommendations; both were addressed in the next round's commit (2ce6708).

ground_truth_defects.description: The ~user allowlist check used per-line grep -qE '^...$', so a multi-line $user whose FIRST line is a valid username passes the check and reaches eval on later lines — a latent (not-yet-reachable) command-injection surface. Non-blocking today because upstream sanitization prevents multi-line input from arriving, but defense-in-depth hardening was recommended.

The new prefix-stripping fix for the pr-create gate closed four flagged bypass shapes but the completeness of the fix against the full prefix-shape space wasn't independently verified in this round — flagged as a MEDIUM completeness gap to re-check next round.

entries.notes: Spike #825 row R4 (GOOD_CATCH): a fluency review that surfaced real, later-confirmed issues while still correctly approving (neither issue was blocking at this commit). Verdict text was 'APPROVED (security) — no BLOCKING finding'.

oracle.established_by: The check-then-strip fix Hakim requested landed in the very next commit (80c62a159), and Hakim's round-3 re-review confirmed it closed.

ground_truth_defects.description: The prefix-strip loop's shape-4 pattern strips a quote-free leading segment ending in a top-level separator (&&, ;, |) — but a GENUINE 'gh pr create' invocation with quote-free args before its own trailing '&& echo done' matches this shape too, so the loop consumes the real create verb as a 'prefix', the head-anchored check fails, and the gate silently SKIPS validation (exit 0). A regression of the exact class the PR set out to fix.

entries.notes: Spike #825 row R5 (GOOD_CATCH) — the paired counterpart to rex-792-2ce6708 in the Rex corpus: Rex's code review of the IDENTICAL commit said APPROVED and missed exactly this defect. This pair is the canonical demonstration of why the eval harness scores per-agent, not per-diff — the same diff, two different agents, two different outcomes.

oracle.established_by: The forge-aware fix for the glab raw-API merge shape landed in the very next commit (be732ce4c).

ground_truth_defects.description: The merge-gate hardening closed 'gh pr merge', 'gh api .../pulls/<N>/merge', and 'glab mr merge', but left 'glab api .../merge_requests/<N>/merge' (the raw REST passthrough) ungated — the #47 silent-bypass class, re-opened on the GitLab side.

entries.notes: Same commit as rex-767-a99f1009 in the Rex corpus: Rex's code review of this identical commit APPROVED and missed this HIGH finding; Hakim's security review caught it the same day. A second same-diff cross-agent pair.

oracle.established_by: No later review or fix on this file ever contradicted the approval. An advisory security pass on a PreToolUse governance gate with no injection/code-exec surface.

entries.notes: Spike #825 row R17 (GOOD_CLEAN). Verdict text was 'NON-BLOCKING (clean on injection; one minor governance note)' — mapped to COMMENT since no changes were required.
